Drolvarg Mantle  
 

Lore Item
Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 7
AGI: +6
SV FIRE: +5
WT: 1.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL except HIE HEF
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Drolvarg Mantle
Lore Item
Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 7
AGI: +6
SV FIRE: +5
WT: 1.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL except HIE HEF
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:1 pp 8 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:24
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-11 23:53:44
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: No Data Pricing Data...
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 48

View User Forum

I was in FV today visiting my friend Shrub Marwood when scanning through his wares I came upon a Drolvarg Mantle. Shrub's selling price? A hansome 2pp and change. Needless to say I scooped it up for my budding monk and went on about my business.

QuoteReply
# Dec 25 2001 at 6:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249876136
34 posts
This must be an always drop of pretty close to it, I camped the room with some friends off and on for 2 days, and everytime the captain popped, he dropped this evertime and nothing else. I guess rare really mean rare. Still waiting on my Lupine Dagger. heh

Sembrin Bowhunter
50 Ranger Luclin
▲ Page top
The TRUE AC of +AGI items
QuoteReply
# Sep 05 2001 at 5:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764498150
3 posts
I have the Drolvarg Mantle on my level 30 SK at the moment, and I must say that it’s worth more than the 7 AC.

Nearly everyone knows that AGI = AC, and shouldn’t be discounted when considering purchasing an item. It seems to me that items with +AGI, usually something higher than 2, actually has a higher total AC than other pieces of armor. When you consider that this hot little number has + 6 AGI, you must realize that the total AC is MUCH higher than the 7 AC on paper. In fact, this mantle actually provides me with 15AC!! (I have taken the item off time and time again to verify that my eyes were not deceiving me.) I foolishly purchased a Fine Plate Pauldron thinking that it would give me a minor AC boost. Well, to my dismay, this item provided a paltry 13 AC. (The “paper” AC is 10, but there is always an AC increase depending upon certain variables) Well, I sold my Fine Plate Pauldron and continue to wear my little mantle with pride.
▲ Page top
RE: The TRUE AC of +AGI items
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2002 at 9:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1613953023551
99 posts
Another cool thing is the SvF bonus. If you want to equip a couple pieces of Coterie armor (if you're a druid or rogue, for instance), you can offset the SvF penalties with this mantle while keeping good AC.

I'm a firm believer that what high-levels ditch at vendors as "crap" can be pretty worthwhile for low- to mid-levels if you balance out penalties and use items that dock stats you don't care about.

Ulthork Hide armor, Coterie armor, Validus Custodus plate, and Giant Scalemail are cheap and generally easy to attain. A few choice pieces of stat/resist jewelry and you're in business.
▲ Page top
RE: The TRUE AC of +AGI items
QuoteReply
# May 30 2002 at 1:51 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591771604173
Sage
85 posts
Actually, the way AC is displayed on your character info panel is screwy and a bit deceptive. There's actually two different kinds of AC: damage mitagation AC (which determines how much damage take when you get it... it's possible to mitigate damage down to 0, in which case they show up as misses), and damage avoidance AC (this is what agility affects... it helps determine whether or not you get hit, along with damage avoidance skills like Dodge, Riposte and Parry).

Generally, the AC boost your get from AGI, isn't worth as much as the AC you get from straight AC boosting items/spells. Also...

There is a "threshhold" for how much your AGI affects your displayed AC. If you have AGI under 75, you take an AC penalty. I think that may be why you see a 15 point AC difference from just 6 points of agility.

Personally, I'd rather go with straight AC, HP, or resist buffing items (the +5 SVF on this is decent) than AGI boosting ones. Engraved Bone Pauldrons and Crested Spaulders are two nice shoulder items that one might want to pick over this particular piece.
▲ Page top
RE: The TRUE AC of +AGI items
QuoteReply
# Nov 29 2001 at 12:36 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249495182
101 posts
drolvarg mantles are practiacally free in some places too! kinda sad tho... people spend so much time and effort to work on their trade skills and they recieve little reward... however i do take great pride when i can fix a steak outa any beast in Norrath lol and being able to make bandaids in the wild is a very good survival technique! well enough of my chatting =P


i personally cant wait till the day when i proudly hold up that steelskin cap and /shout "w00t w00t.... w00t w00t....w00t w00t!!!!!!!!! I L337 I R0X0R T/-\Ilor d00d!!!"

▲ Page top
Merchant
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2001 at 8:39 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
I ran across one of these at a merchant when I was way new to the game, and bought for under 10 plat. Was this just some sort of fluke? I've still got it, but am using Adamantite epaulets now, as I am a cleric and Wis is more important than Agi and sv fire. I've yet to see another magic item thats any good priced so low!
▲ Page top
RE: Merchant
QuoteReply
# Dec 14 2001 at 10:53 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249475779
Scholar
148 posts
No, it is not you. There are many items that merchants sell for far more than a player would consider spending (Fine Steel anyone?). This sells from a merchant for 2-3pp. A player will ask 75-100p. A similar thing is an Archeologist pack (weight reducing bag), merchants sell for 2p, players sell for several hundred. Helm of Rile sells player to player for about 100-300p, merchants ask 10,000p.
▲ Page top
RE: Merchant
QuoteReply
# Jul 02 2001 at 4:58 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761913405
67 posts
It seems to be just one of those weird pricing flukes but by Brell's Beard I am a happy Dwarf! Just the other day I happened to find one of these babies being sold by an NPC vendor in South Kaladim (in the leather armor store across from Irontoe's Eats) for less than 2 and a half platinum. WOOT!

Why can't some races wear this?!
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2001 at 4:43 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It's not all forms of elves that cannot wear it. Dark elves can wear it. So that would rule that out. It seems that for some reason High Elves, Wood Elves, and Half Elves have been left of the list, even though there are races or large, medium, and small on the list and even a member of the elven race. Why would they not allow the other three elven races to wear it??

Doesn't make any sense.
▲ Page top
RE: Why can't some races wear this?!
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2001 at 11:07 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247994243
Scholar
78 posts
I was just thinking the same thing actually. Moreover, the item doesn't really have a theme or anything, like an item bearing a particular race's name, or god... or anything. It's just a drolvarg mantle, dropped from a drolvarg...

<--- me thinks Verant drew wearable races out of a hat for an item they knew anyone hunting in this zone wouldn't care about anyway.

On Tunare we calls it vendor-bait.
